Title: Recent progress and new perspectives for diterpenoid biosynthesis in medicinal plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used commercially for treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and several heart ailments
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports forskolin biosynthesis in heterologous reconstruction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfPOR was co-expressed with CYP76AH15, CYP76AH11, CYP76AH16, and CfACT1-8 in yeast engineered for 13R-manoyl oxide production. 2. This combination enabled heterologous production of forskolin. 3. CfPOR serves as redox partner for P450-dependent oxidation steps.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics and metabolomics are discussed broadly in the review, but no accession for CfPOR is provided
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful helper gene for reconstructing P450-intensive forskolin pathway in yeast
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; CfPOR is included because it is directly used in pathway reconstitution but is not a pathway enzyme for carbon skeleton formation or terminal decoration product class assignment beyond electron transfer.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Probable intermediate in ingenol mebutate biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil-derived macrocyclic diterpenoid pathway context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of casbene-derived oxidized intermediates to jolkinol C in transient expression system
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIADH1 was identified with two P450s as involved in conversion of casbene to jolkinol C. 2. Coexpression with EICBS, CYP71D445, and CYP726A27 in Nicotiana benthamiana yielded jolkinol C. 3. It functions after P450 oxidation steps in macrocyclic diterpenoid pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics of Euphorbia species; no accession provided
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for heterologous production of jolkinol C and downstream ingenol mebutate-related compounds
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homolog EpADH1 was identified in Euphorbia peplus.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Probable intermediate in ingenol mebutate biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Homologous ADH candidate for casbene-to-jolkinol C pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpADH1 was identified as a homolog of EIADH1 by comparative transcriptomics between Euphorbia lathyris and Euphorbia peplus. 2. It is proposed to participate in macrocyclic diterpenoid biosynthesis toward jolkinol C/ingenol-type products.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate accessory gene for reconstructing Euphorbia macrocyclic diterpenoid pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional detail is less explicit than for EIADH1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to 8α-hydroxy-copalyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates forskolin-specific carbon skeleton biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfTPS2 synthesizes 8α-hydroxy-CPP from GGPP. 2. This intermediate is then converted by CfTPS3 to 13R-manoyl oxide en route to forskolin. 3. It is a core upstream enzyme in the elucidated forskolin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro assay; in planta assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme for reconstructing forskolin pathway in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper summarizing Pateraki et al. findings.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 8α-hydroxy-CPP to 13R-manoyl oxide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces 13R-manoyl oxide precursor for forskolin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfTPS3 acts downstream of CfTPS2 to form 13R-manoyl oxide. 2. It is essential for forskolin skeleton biosynthesis. 3. CfTPS3 can also pair with CfTPS1 or CfTPS4 in miltiradiene-related metabolism context.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro assay; in planta assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key skeleton-forming enzyme for forskolin production systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also participates in miltiradiene formation with CPP-producing enzymes in C. forskohlii.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes early oxidation toward forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH8 is one of the CYP76AH8/15/17 enzymes that convert 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide. 2. It participates in the elucidated oxidative branch toward forskolin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate oxidation module for pathway engineering of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review groups CYP76AH8/15/17 together for this step.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide and can hydroxylate miltiradiene to ferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes key oxidation step toward forskolin and also links to ferruginol formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH15 converts 13R-manoyl oxide into 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide in forskolin biosynthesis. 2. It also hydroxylates miltiradiene to ferruginol in C. forskohlii abietane metabolism. 3. It was included in yeast co-expression that yielded forskolin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value P450 target for engineering forskolin and ferruginol-related diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutagenesis improved intermediate formation but end-product forskolin remained low in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed again in protein engineering section for improved forskolin pathway flux.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alternative P450 for early oxidation toward forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH17 is grouped with CYP76AH8/15 for conversion of 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide. 2. It represents catalytic redundancy/promiscuity in the forskolin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative oxidation enzyme for modular pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Redundant with CYP76AH8/15 in the first oxidation step.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multifunctionally oxidizes 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide to 9-deoxy-7-deacetylforskolin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Carries out key multifunctional oxidation toward forskolin core maturation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH11 converts 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide to 9-deoxy-7-deacetylforskolin. 2. It is a multifunctional P450 in the elucidated forskolin pathway. 3. Yeast co-expression including CYP76AH11 enabled forskolin production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for reconstructing the late oxidative steps of forskolin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as multifunctional in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 9-deoxy-7-deacetylforskolin to 7-deacetylforskolin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes late oxidation toward forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH16 converts 9-deoxy-7-deacetylforskolin into 7-deacetylforskolin. 2. It is one of the final oxidative enzymes before acetylation to forskolin. 3. It functioned in the yeast-reconstructed pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required late-pathway oxidation step for full forskolin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates 7-deacetylforskolin at C7 to form forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of glaucoma, asthma, and heart ailments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces forskolin by terminal acetylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfACT1-8 acetylates 7-deacetylforskolin at C7 to obtain forskolin. 2. Co-expression with CfPOR and three P450s in yeast enabled forskolin production. 3. It belongs to BAHD acyltransferase clade III.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Terminal enzyme for complete heterologous biosynthesis of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes CfACT1-8 family placement and broad acyltransferase context.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to copalyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides CPP precursor for miltiradiene biosynthesis in C. forskohlii
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfTPS1 catalyzes GGPP to CPP. 2. CPP can be further cyclized by CfTPS3 or CfTPS4 to form miltiradiene. 3. This links C. forskohlii to abietane diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supplying enzyme for abietane diterpenoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not part of main forskolin route.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes CPP to miltiradiene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces miltiradiene precursor in C. forskohlii
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfTPS4 converts CPP to miltiradiene in C. forskohlii. 2. Miltiradiene is hydroxylated by CYP76AH15 to ferruginol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ferruginol/abietane branches in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CfTPS3 may also form miltiradiene from CPP.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supplies ent-CPP intermediate for andrographolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApCPS1 catalyzes GGPP to ent-CPP. 2. ent-CPP was proposed as precursor of andrographolides. 3. In planta verification for andrographolide route remains incomplete.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; in vitro assay; genome-scale functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Chromosome-scale genome and transcriptome sequencing discussed; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream enzyme for engineering andrographolide production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review notes lack of direct in planta verification for full pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially contributes to tissue-specific accumulation of ent-labdane diterpenes and andrographolide precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApCPS2 was originally suggested to be involved in tissue-specific accumulation of ent-labdane-related diterpenes. 2. Later work demonstrated that ApCPS2 catalyzes GGPP to ent-CPP. 3. Its role in andrographolide biosynthesis remains probable rather than fully proven.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tissue-specific expression analysis; in vitro assay; genome functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome and transcriptome sequencing; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point enzyme for ent-labdane pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete knowledge of downstream steps limits application
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Earlier attribution as core andrographolide synthase was revised by later functional work.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to copalyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expands diterpene scaffold diversity in A. paniculata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApCPS3 produces CPP rather than ent-CPP. 2. It contributes to diversified labdane-related diterpene scaffolds but is not specifically assigned to andrographolide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be used to broaden diterpene scaffold production in synthetic biology
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly linked to target metabolite andrographolide
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review treats it as a key characterized pathway enzyme in the species.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-glycosylation of andrograpanin to form neoandrographolide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly converts andrograpanin to neoandrographolide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApUGT74 catalyzes O-glycosylation of andrograpanin. 2. This reaction yields neoandrographolide, a major active product in A. paniculata.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome and transcriptome sequencing; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal glycosyltransferase for producing active glycosylated diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states ApUGT can glycosylate many natural and synthetic compounds, indicating promiscuity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-glycosylation of andrograpanin to form neoandrographolide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly contributes to neoandrographolide formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApUGT73AU1 glycosylates andrograpanin to yield neoandrographolide. 2. It is one of two A. paniculata UGTs functionally linked to this active diterpenoid glycoside.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome and transcriptome sequencing; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative or complementary UGT for glycosylated diterpenoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bifunctional class I/class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly converts GGPP to levopimaradiene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Treatment of cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases; protection of central nervous system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides proposed precursor for ginkgolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbLPS converts GGPP into levopimaradiene. 2. Levopimaradiene is proposed as the tricyclic precursor that undergoes dehydrogenation, oxidation, and rearrangement to form ginkgolides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA library screening; expression in Escherichia coli; enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library-based cloning; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gateway enzyme for heterologous production of ginkgolide precursor levopimaradiene
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downstream oxidation and rearrangement steps remain unclear
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The pathway beyond levopimaradiene is unresolved.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 16-hydroxylation of peregrinol to generate labd-13Z-ene-9,15,16-triol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces potential intermediate for downstream furan/lactone diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76BK1 performs 16-hydroxylation of peregrinol. 2. The product labd-13Z-ene-9,15,16-triol is a potential intermediate in furan- and lactone-containing diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful oxidation enzyme for generating specialized labdane derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as first diterpene synthase step prior to MvELS in marrubiin-related pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates precursor formation for epoxy-labdane intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MvCPS1 functions together with MvELS before CYP71AU87 action. 2. Their products are converted into 9,13-epoxy labd-14-ene-18/19-ol isomers, probable intermediates in marrubiin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream enzyme for marrubiin pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Immediate product not specified in the review excerpt
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: substrate for CYP71AU87 yielding 9,13-epoxy labd-14-ene-18/19-ol isomers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts with MvCPS1 to generate diterpene substrate for CYP71AU87
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces intermediate scaffold for CYP71AU87-dependent epoxy products
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MvELS acts after MvCPS1 in the marrubiin-related pathway. 2. The sequential diTPS reactions generate substrate for CYP71AU87 to form epoxy labdane isomers.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for generating marrubiin pathway intermediates in synthetic systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of isomeric 9,13-epoxy labd-14-ene-18/19-ol products after sequential diTPS reactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates probable intermediates for marrubiin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AU87 converts products of MvCPS1 and MvELS into 9,13-epoxy labd-14-ene-18/19-ol isomers. 2. These isomers may be intermediates in marrubiin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate oxidation enzyme for marrubiin pathway elucidation and engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downstream conversion to marrubiin remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to CPP for subsequent conversion to miltiradiene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates tanshinone carbon skeleton biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmCPS1 catalyzes the first committed diterpene cyclization from GGPP toward miltiradiene. 2. It works with SmKSL1 to produce the tanshinone precursor miltiradiene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; isotope-feeding context for pathway assignment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative omics used for downstream enzyme discovery; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core upstream gene for tanshinone metabolic engineering in hairy roots and yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coexpression with SmHMGR and SmGGPPS enhanced tanshinones in hairy roots, but those genes are outside core pathway focus here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes CPP to miltiradiene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces miltiradiene carbon skeleton of tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmKSL1 acts after SmCPS1 to produce miltiradiene. 2. Labeled feeding showed miltiradiene is decorated into cryptotanshinone, confirming its precursor role.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key skeleton-forming enzyme for tanshinone production platforms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in modular pathway engineering in yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts miltiradiene to ferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential early oxidation step in tanshinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH1 converts miltiradiene to ferruginol. 2. RNAi analysis showed it is a crucial enzyme in downstream tanshinone biosynthesis. 3. Structural analysis and mutagenesis later informed functional integration with CYP76AH3-like activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; functional characterization; RNAi in hairy roots; structural analysis and mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expression correlated with tanshinone accumulation in different tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority target for tanshinone pathway reconstruction and P450 engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation of residues 301 and 479 improved combined activities.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ferruginol at two different carbon positions generating substrates for CYP76AK1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives pathway bifurcation and diversification toward tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH3 oxidizes ferruginol at two carbon sites. 2. Its substrate promiscuity contributes to pathway bifurcation en route to tanshinones. 3. Structural comparison with CYP76AH1 informed catalytic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative omics; functional characterization; structural analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified based on comparative omics linked to tanshinone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for generating specific oxidized tanshinone intermediates; target for protein engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity can generate pathway bifurcation and mixed products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the critical oxidation nodes in tanshinone pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the C20 intermediate using two different substrates produced by CYP76AH3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Continues oxidation sequence and contributes to tanshinone pathway bifurcation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AK1 hydroxylates C20 intermediates generated by CYP76AH3. 2. It accepts two substrates, contributing to pathway bifurcation and diversity toward tanshinones.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified based on comparative omics linked to tanshinone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for producing downstream tanshinone intermediates in engineered systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts sequentially after CYP76AH3.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation and heterocyclization contributing to D-ring formation of tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for ring formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables D-ring formation in tanshinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D373 was identified from a P450 tandem gene array. 2. It catalyzes hydroxylation and heterocyclization to form the D-ring of tanshinones.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical late-pathway enzyme for producing mature tanshinone skeletons
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tandemly arrayed P450 genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation and heterocyclization contributing to D-ring formation of tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for ring formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables D-ring formation in tanshinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D375 was identified from a P450 tandem gene array. 2. It catalyzes hydroxylation and heterocyclization to form the D-ring of tanshinones.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical late-pathway enzyme for reconstructing mature tanshinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tandemly arrayed P450 genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ferruginol to 11-hydroxy ferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidative, anticancer, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial; potential neurodegenerative disorder therapeutics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes early oxidation toward carnosic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH4 can produce 11-hydroxy ferruginol, similar to CYP76AH3. 2. It functions in the pathway leading to carnosic acid and carnosol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate oxidation module for producing antioxidant diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Species assignment is generalized in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidize ferruginol to 11-hydroxy ferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidative, anticancer, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial; potential neurodegenerative disorder therapeutics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contribute early oxidation toward carnosic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH22-24 are also able to produce 11-hydroxy ferruginol. 2. They provide functional redundancy in the carnosic acid branch.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative enzymes for pathway tuning and redundancy in metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped enzymes in review; not individually resolved here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Perform three sequential C20 oxidations converting 11-hydroxy ferruginol to carnosic acid; can also oxidize miltiradiene and ferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidative, anticancer, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial; likely intermediates for other diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generate carnosic acid and also support pisiferic acid branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AK6-8 sequentially oxidize 11-hydroxy ferruginol to carnosic acid. 2. These enzymes can also oxidize miltiradiene and ferruginol, the latter leading to pisiferic acid. 3. Carnosic acid can spontaneously oxidize to carnosol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key terminal oxidation set for production of antioxidant abietane diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate promiscuity may generate multiple branch products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Carnosol arises spontaneously from carnosic acid.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ferruginol at position 2α to produce salviol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces salviol from ferruginol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71BE52 oxidizes ferruginol at position 2α. 2. This reaction forms salviol in Salvia pomifera.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Specialized branch enzyme for salviol production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to CPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies CPP precursor for miltiradiene formation in triptolide pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functional characterization and RNAi showed that TwTPS7v2 can use GGPP to produce CPP. 2. CPP is further cyclized by TwTPS27v2 to miltiradiene, precursor of triptolide.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from suspension cells producing triptolide under induction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induction in suspension cell cultures producing triptolide
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome, genome, and metabolomic integration; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core upstream enzyme for triptolide pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TwTPS9v2 is alternative CPP synthase.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to CPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alternative CPP-forming enzyme in triptolide pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwTPS9v2 can also produce CPP from GGPP. 2. It provides an alternative upstream route to miltiradiene in T. wilfordii.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from suspension cells producing triptolide under induction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induction in suspension cell cultures producing triptolide
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome, genome, and metabolomic integration; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative upstream gene for tuning triptolide precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene synthase; TPS-b subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes CPP to miltiradiene, which spontaneously forms abietatriene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates gateway precursor skeleton for triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwTPS27v2 cyclizes CPP to miltiradiene. 2. Miltiradiene spontaneously forms abietatriene and serves as precursor of triptolide.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome analysis; functional characterization; RNAi; feeding study context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induction in suspension cell cultures producing triptolide
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome, genome, metabolomic integration; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gateway skeleton-forming enzyme for triptolide production platforms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In prior sections also noted as TwTPS-27 of TPS-b subfamily.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes a methyl group to the acid moiety of dehydroabietic acid in the triptolide pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes late oxidation toward triptolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP728B70 was identified by integrating genomic, transcriptomic, and metabolomic data. 2. Synthetic biology, RNAi, and overexpression demonstrated that it oxidizes a methyl group to the acid moiety of dehydroabietic acid in triptolide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome/transcriptome/metabolome integration; synthetic biology; RNAi; overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genomic, transcriptomic, and metabolomic data integration; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical late-pathway enzyme for constructing triptolide-producing systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Remaining pathway steps are still unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four candidate P450s linked to triptolide biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ent-kaurenoic acid at C13 to generate steviol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: branch switch from gibberellin-shared upstream pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sweeteners with nutritional and pharmacological activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Commits ent-kaurenoic acid toward steviol glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrKA13H hydroxylates ent-kaurenoic acid to steviol. 2. This is the divergence point from gibberellin-related metabolism into steviol glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; pathway reconstruction in E. coli discussed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key branch-point gene for steviol glycoside engineering in microbes and plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Last five steps are specific to steviol glycoside biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates steviol at the C13 hydroxyl group to produce steviolmonoside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sweeteners with nutritional and pharmacological activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates steviol glycosylation toward sweet diterpenoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT85C2 preferentially glycosylates the C13 hydroxyl of steviol to produce steviolmonoside. 2. This is the first committed glycosylation in major steviol glycoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; pathway reconstruction in E. coli discussed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core glycosyltransferase for engineering sweetener composition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate flexibility may alter product spectrum
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AkUGT85A58 shows similar substrate flexibility according to the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds glucose to the C2 hydroxyl of the C13 glucose moiety of steviolmonoside to form steviolbiosoide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sweeteners with nutritional and pharmacological activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Extends glycosylation chain toward stevioside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT91D2 glycosylates steviolmonoside to steviolbiosoide. 2. It performs the second glycosylation step in major steviol glycoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports tailoring of steviol glycoside chain length and sweetness profile
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates the C19 hydroxyl/carboxyl moiety of steviolbiosoide to form stevioside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sweetener with nutritional and pharmacological activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces stevioside from steviolbiosoide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT74G1 catalyzes glucosylation at the C19 moiety of steviolbiosoide. 2. This reaction yields stevioside.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for manipulating stevioside levels in production systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates the C3 hydroxyl group of the C13 glucose moiety of stevioside to form rebaudioside A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces rebaudioside A from stevioside
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT76G1 performs terminal glucosylation on stevioside. 2. This reaction forms rebaudioside A, one of the most abundant desirable steviol glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; pathway engineering in E. coli discussed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target to improve quality profile of stevia sweeteners
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates steviol and steviolmonoside on the C19 hydroxyl group to form steviol 19-O-glucoside and rubusoside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits human fructose transporter GLUT5 and glucose transporter GLUT1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes key glycosylation step to produce rubusoside
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT73E1 glycosylates steviol and steviolmonoside at C19. 2. It generates steviol 19-O-glucoside and rubusoside.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for production of rubusoside, a bioactive sweet diterpenoid glycoside
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glycosylation on the C19 hydroxyl group of ent-kaurenoic acid, steviol, and steviolmonoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to C19 glycosylation steps producing rubusoside-related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsUGT75L20 glycosylates multiple C19 substrates in rubusoside biosynthesis. 2. It can generate β-D-glucosyl ent-kauren-19-oate, steviol 19-O-glucoside, and rubusoside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; evolutionary analysis; gene cloning; functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Flexible glycosyltransferase for engineered rubusoside production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate flexibility may create mixed glycoside products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glycosylation on the C19 hydroxyl group of ent-kaurenoic acid, steviol, and steviolmonoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to C19 glycosylation toward rubusoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsUGT75T4 catalyzes C19 glycosylation of ent-kaurenoic acid, steviol, and steviolmonoside. 2. It participates in generating rubusoside-related products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; evolutionary analysis; gene cloning; functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative glycosyltransferase for tailoring rubusoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate flexibility may broaden product profile
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glycosylation on the C19 hydroxyl group of multiple steviol-related substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to C19 glycosylation steps in rubusoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AkUGT75L21 glycosylates ent-kaurenoic acid, steviol, and steviolmonoside at C19. 2. It supports rubusoside formation in A. keiskei.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; evolutionary analysis; gene cloning; functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for heterologous production of rubusoside and related glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glycosylation on the C19 hydroxyl group of multiple steviol-related substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to C19 glycosylation toward rubusoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AkUGT75W2 catalyzes C19 glycosylation of ent-kaurenoic acid, steviol, and steviolmonoside. 2. It supports the biosynthesis of rubusoside-related glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; evolutionary analysis; gene cloning; functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative UGT for synthetic biology of rubusoside
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate promiscuity may alter product ratio
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Serves as 13-O-glycosyltransferase converting steviol 19-O-glucoside to rubusoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces rubusoside by 13-O glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsUGT85A57 acts as a 13-O-glycosyltransferase. 2. It converts steviol 19-O-β-D-glucopyranoside into rubusoside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; evolutionary analysis; gene cloning; functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Terminal UGT for high-value rubusoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as 13-O-glycosyltransferase; can use both steviol and steviol 19-O-glucoside as substrates to form rubusoside-related products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces rubusoside and shows substrate flexibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AkUGT85A58 is a 13-O-glycosyltransferase converting steviol 19-O-glucoside to rubusoside. 2. It also shows substrate flexibility and can use steviol directly, similar to SrUGT85C2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; evolutionary analysis; gene cloning; functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Flexible terminal glycosyltransferase for rubusoside pathway design
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate flexibility may create multiple products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Noted to behave similarly to SrUGT85C2.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to ent-CPP
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent anticancer activity; covalent NLRP3 inhibitor with anti-inflammasome activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides ent-CPP precursor for ent-kaurene/oridonin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IrCPS4 converts GGPP to ent-CPP. 2. ent-CPP is then used by IrKSL5 to form ent-kaurene, likely the carbon skeleton of oridonin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core upstream enzyme for oridonin precursor engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP to ent-CPP
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent anticancer activity; covalent NLRP3 inhibitor with anti-inflammasome activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alternative ent-CPP-forming enzyme for oridonin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IrCPS5 also converts GGPP to ent-CPP. 2. It provides an alternative upstream route to ent-kaurene in oridonin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative enzyme for balancing precursor formation in oridonin pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes ent-CPP to ent-kaurene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces likely carbon skeleton precursor of oridonin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IrKSL5 converts ent-CPP to ent-kaurene. 2. ent-Kaurene is proposed as the carbon skeleton of oridonin and is expected to be modified by P450s.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gateway enzyme for engineering oridonin precursor production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to taxadiene, forming the tricyclic taxane skeleton
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates committed and rate-limiting step of paclitaxel biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TbTS converts GGPP to taxadiene. 2. This reaction forms the taxane core and is considered rate-limiting in paclitaxel synthesis. 3. It is a key engineering target for taxadiene production in E. coli and plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; heterologous production in E. coli and Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primary target for taxadiene and paclitaxel precursor bioengineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downstream P450 steps limit full paclitaxel production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in modular E. coli optimization and chloroplast engineering in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxadiene at C5 and causes double-bond migration to form taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Performs first oxidative modification of taxadiene toward paclitaxel
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP725A4/T5αOH converts taxadiene to taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol. 2. The reaction involves hydroxylation and carbon double-bond migration. 3. Introducing P450-CPR fusion in E. coli caused low yields and by-product accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential P450 for early paclitaxel pathway reconstruction, though difficult to express efficiently in microbes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disturbs metabolic flux in E. coli and leads to by-products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as taxadiene-5α-hydroxylase.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the 5α-hydroxy group of taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms acetylated taxane intermediate in one proposed reaction order
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TAT acetylates taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol to form its acetate. 2. This is one of the proposed early routes toward 2-debenzoyltaxane.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization in paclitaxel pathway studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Acyltransferase module for taxane pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overall reaction order in early paclitaxel pathway remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two alternative sequences discussed.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates acetylated taxane intermediate at C10β
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds C10β hydroxyl during paclitaxel biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T10βOH hydroxylates taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-yl acetate to form 5α-acetoxytaxadien-10β-diol. 2. It belongs to the CYP725A1 family.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important oxidation step for advanced taxane engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxane intermediate at C13α to form taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α,13α-diol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports one proposed early hydroxylation route in paclitaxel biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T13αH catalyzes hydroxylation at C13α to form taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α,13α-diol. 2. The order of this step relative to acetylation remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate step for testing alternative paclitaxel pathway orders in synthetic reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes uncertainty in reaction order.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP directly to casbene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial source context for ingenol mebutate-related products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces first committed intermediate casbene toward macrocyclic diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EeTPS2 is a casbene synthase identified from Euphorbiaceae. 2. Casbene is considered the first committed intermediate toward complex macrocyclic diterpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful starter enzyme for producing macrocyclic diterpene scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of a broader Euphorbiaceae comparative analysis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces casbene committed intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HnTPS4 is one of the Euphorbiaceae-associated casbene synthases reported to generate casbene. 2. Casbene is regarded as gateway intermediate to more complex macrocyclic diterpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful committed-step enzyme for scaffold production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Species assignment follows review text, though taxonomy may warrant external checking.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces casbene gateway precursor
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ErTPS6 is a casbene synthase identified from Euphorbiaceae. 2. It contributes to the comparative evidence that casbene is a first committed intermediate of macrocyclic diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Committed-step enzyme for macrocyclic diterpene engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces casbene gateway precursor
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsTPS10 is one of the characterized casbene synthases from Euphorbiaceae-related comparison. 2. It supports the role of casbene as the first committed intermediate in macrocyclic diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gateway enzyme for macrocyclic diterpene synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces casbene and anchors the characterized Euphorbiaceae gene cluster
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcCAS3 is a casbene synthase from R. communis. 2. It is located within a physical gene cluster containing neocembrene synthase, CYP726A P450s, and ADHs. 3. Casbene is considered the first committed intermediate in complex macrocyclic diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome/gene cluster analysis; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key entry enzyme for reconstructing clustered macrocyclic diterpenoid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Physical clustering supports pathway gene discovery.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 5-oxidation of casbene to produce 5-keto-casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates oxidative tailoring of casbene in macrocyclic diterpenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP726A14 catalyzes 5-oxidation of casbene to 5-keto-casbene. 2. It is part of the R. communis diterpenoid biosynthetic gene cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cluster analysis; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful P450 for generating oxidized casbene intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of several CYP726As in the cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 5-oxidation of casbene to produce 5-keto-casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to oxidative conversion of casbene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP726A17 catalyzes 5-oxidation of casbene to 5-keto-casbene. 2. It is one of the clustered P450s in the R. communis macrocyclic diterpenoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cluster analysis; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative oxidation module for casbene pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 5-oxidation of casbene to produce 5-keto-casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to oxidative conversion of casbene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP726A18 catalyzes 5-oxidation of casbene to 5-keto-casbene. 2. It is one of the clustered P450s implicated in macrocyclic diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cluster analysis; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative oxidation module for casbene-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 7,8-epoxidation of 5-keto-casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces epoxidized casbene intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP726A16 uses 5-keto-casbene as substrate. 2. It catalyzes 7,8-epoxidation to form 5-keto-7,8-epoxy-casbene.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cluster analysis; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful late oxidation/epoxidation module in casbene pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses GGPP as substrate to produce neocembrene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces neocembrene branch precursor in clustered macrocyclic diterpenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcCAS2 converts GGPP to neocembrene. 2. It is part of the R. communis macrocyclic diterpenoid gene cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cluster analysis; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables alternative macrocyclic diterpene scaffold production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes neocembrene to 5-keto-neocembrene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces oxidation into neocembrene branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP726A15 oxidizes neocembrene produced by RcCAS2. 2. The product is 5-keto-neocembrene.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cluster analysis; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for oxidized cembrane product generation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides casbene precursor in reconstructed jolkinol C pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EICBS was coexpressed with CYP71D445, CYP726A27, and EIADH1 in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. This combination yielded jolkinol C.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; in planta transient co-expression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic analysis of mature seeds; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Entry enzyme for heterologous production of jolkinol C and ingenol-related diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transient expression performed in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 9-oxidation of casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces 9-oxidation needed for jolkinol C formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D445 catalyzes 9-oxidation of casbene. 2. Coexpression with EICBS, CYP726A27, and EIADH1 led to rearrangement/cyclization yielding jolkinol C.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic analysis of mature seeds; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core oxidation gene for ingenol-related pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homolog CYP71D365 identified in E. peplus.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 5-hydroxylation of casbene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces 5-hydroxylation required for jolkinol C production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP726A27 catalyzes 5-hydroxylation of casbene. 2. Coexpression with EICBS, CYP71D445, and EIADH1 in Nicotiana benthamiana yielded jolkinol C.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic analysis of mature seeds; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core P450 for synthetic production of jolkinol C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homolog CYP726A4 identified in E. peplus.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted/identified homologous casbene synthase for macrocyclic diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Homologous committed-step enzyme in E. peplus pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpCBS was identified as a homolog of EICBS by comparative transcriptomics. 2. It is proposed to function in E. peplus macrocyclic diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate entry enzyme for E. peplus pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Homolog of CYP71D445 implicated in casbene oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Homologous oxidation module in E. peplus pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D365 was identified as a homolog of CYP71D445 in E. peplus. 2. It is proposed to participate in casbene oxidation toward ingenol-related diterpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate oxidation gene for E. peplus pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Less directly validated than E. lathyris homologs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Homolog of CYP726A27 implicated in casbene hydroxylation/oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Homologous oxidation module in E. peplus pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP726A4 was identified as a homolog of CYP726A27 in E. peplus. 2. It is proposed to participate in oxidation of casbene in ingenol-related pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate P450 for E. peplus macrocyclic diterpenoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Less directly validated than E. lathyris homologs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
